title: Dragon Grove

borrow: eggs and tiny dragons as readable rewards
restoring a magical clearing as repeated objective
avoid: Merge Dragons branding, art, names, maps, exact economy, and UI copy
broad collection, timers, monetization, or late-game pacing

player_verbs: read the reward and next blocked/restored goal
rules: The first slice uses a 5x5 logical Y-up grid.
Three level-1 eggs merge into one level-2 hatchling.
Three level-1 sprouts merge into one level-2 bloom.
Each successful merge restores one shadowed grove tile.
If fewer than three matching objects exist, the UI shows a blocked reason.
feedback: The merged tile changes icon/color/tier immediately.
A message names what merged and what was restored.
HUD counters update for restored tiles and merge count.
The next merge-ready group is highlighted.
risk: Store-page quick check is not enough for final pacing or visual parity.
Procedural shapes are acceptable only as prototype/debug visuals.
The first build can feel like a debug grid unless the action/reward text is clear.
goals: make the next merge action obvious within five seconds
replay_reason: The player repeats short merges to restore more of the grove and reveal stronger dragon objects.
first_slice_acceptance: native build opens to one grove screen
the first action merges three objects
Y-up grid coordinates are internal and documented
HUD shows goal, restored progress, and next action
blocked/no-merge state has a readable reason
